title: 8 Misdirected Funds Recovery
body_text:
8	Misdirected Funds Recovery
8.1	Overview of Misdirect Funds Recovery
When
Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
for Business is implemented, its Account Number Routing (ANR) feature raises a possibility that a payment may be misdirected to an unintended recipient by entering an account number incorrectly.
To assist in the recovery of these misdirected funds,
Interac
Â®
has prepared an interim process called Misdirected Funds Recovery to allow the financial institution sending funds through ANR to request the return of funds that were sent in error. This feature is only available for ANR transactions.
Under the Misdirected Funds Recovery process, a sending financial institution (FI) is allowed to create a request for the receiving FI to return the funds. The request initiates an email sent to the receiving FI advising them of the request. The receiving FI can then log in to OAS, access the Misdirected Funds Recovery request, and enter the amount they will return (if any). A return email is sent back to the requesting FI describing the recoverable amount. The actual return of funds is handled between the requesting and responding FIs outside of OAS or the
Interac
e-Transfer system.
This process is intended solely for the recovery of funds sent in error through ANR; it is
NOT
intended for the recovery of funds for any other reasons, including fraudulent transactions.
8.2	Initiating a Misdirect Funds Recovery Request
To initiate a Misdirected Funds Recovery request:
Open the Transfer Details page of the ANR transfer that was sent to the wrong account. See Section 4.3, Tracing
Interac
e-Transfer Transactions in OAS, for details on opening a Transfer Details screen or a Customer Details screen.
Select
Recover Misdirected Funds
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Recover Misdirected Funds
Complete the Funds Recovery Details page, then select
Submit
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: The Funds Recovery Details Page
Confirm that a Misdirected Funds Recovery request was successfully submitted. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Successful Misdirected Funds Recovery Request Submitted
An email is sent to the receiving FI on the successful submission of the Misdirected Funds Recovery request. Draft or Submitted Misdirected Funds Recovery requests appear on the Misdirected Funds Recovery tab in a section called Funds Recovery. Only one Misdirected Funds Recovery request can be sent per ANR transaction. Initiating FIs can optionally upload supporting documentation that can be seen by the receiving FI when they review incoming requests.
8.3	Responding to a Misdirected Funds Recovery Request
To respond to a Misdirected Funds Recovery request:
Locate the Misdirected Funds Recovery request in the Misdirected funds section of the Misdirected Funds Recovery tab in OAS. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Received Misdirected Funds Recovery Requests
Select the
Recovery ID
link against the Misdirected Funds Recovery request to open the Funds Recovery Details page.
Review the request details on the Funds Recovery Details page, including any documents that the initiating FI may have attached to support the request.
Update the Recovered Funds field of the Funds Recovery Details page with the amount of funds being recovered and add any optional supporting documentation. Select
Submit
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Recovered Funds Amount
When the receiving FI re-submits the Misdirected Funds Recovery request, complete with a Recovered Funds amount, a notification email is sent back to the initiating FI disclosing amount of the funds recoverable.
This re-submission of the Misdirected Funds Recovery request by the receiving FI marks the close of that Misdirected Funds Recovery request, which disappears from the Funds Recovery sections of Misdirected Funds Recovery tab of the both the initiating and receiving FIs.
A permanent record of the recovery of the funds is marked against the original ANR transaction in OAS, visible to both parties if they look at the Transfer Details page of the transaction. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Transfer Details with a Recovered Amount
The FIs must work outside of OAS or
Interac
e-Transfer to move the recovered funds between their institutions, as per their standing processes for recovering funds from other FIs.
END
